hooks.notes: The Ledger recognizes Rowan unusually fast after the rescue incident

hooks.expectedPayoff: Identify the source and meaning of the metallic bell sounding from forbidden depth
hooks.notes: On-page origin established: Rowan feels a second resonance beyond normal stone failure, then hears a metallic bell from deeper than any legal shaft; he becomes certain the open space ahead is old and wrongly connected to this level.

hooks.expectedPayoff: Establish whether Derren Holt, Benn, and any other lower survivors remain alive and reachable
hooks.notes: After the split in the galleries and later collapse, Rowan has no information about Derren, Benn, or the rest of the mine below.

hooks.expectedPayoff: Discover what lies in the old open space ahead and whether it offers escape, deeper danger, or both
hooks.notes: The collapse exposes airflow and a route toward open space below and ahead, but Rowan cannot tell whether it is a vent path or a drop into deeper levels.

hooks.expectedPayoff: Reveal the nature, source, and limits of Rowan's abnormal stone-sense/echo-sense
hooks.notes: Rowan's structural intuition is explicitly described and materially saves him and Lio before the wall/roof failure, but he does not understand what it is.

hooks.expectedPayoff: Clarify how Pike Holdings can exploit Elias Vale's winter-sickness debt and whether Rowan can earn enough recognition or leverage to resist it
hooks.notes: Chapter 1 makes the family debt immediate and concrete on-page, with Rowan explicitly motivated by rescue pay and witness recognition because official records can erase unlicensed labor.

Pike Holdings is concretely present at South Third through Joren Pike's banner and guild-side control at the shaft mouth; Rowan also explicitly links Pike to his father's debt and fears body-count logic over names.
A salvage token from the collapse bears a defaced symbol tied to the Bell Vault
Lin Vey sees Rowan as useful but unproven; alliance depends on shared risk, not trust
Monster movement patterns suggest something deeper is displacing them upward
Brass Lantern’s hazard reports around Brackenside have been selectively altered
Rowan receives the first hint of a path evolution related to listening, routes, and resonance
Tessa Quill suspects multiple frontier debts are being bundled to force settlement seizure
An infected creature carries spore growth not seen in upper layers before
Serik Dane marks Rowan as a future problem after a public claim dispute
Rowan learns his mother did not die on a routine collapse after all
The flooded galleries distort sound in ways that make Rowan’s sense stronger rather than weaker
Brother Cal implies Rowan may be compatible with a sealed deep interface
Proof emerges that Brackenside sits over a structure valuable enough to trigger open faction conflict
